{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider problem (16.3), and suppose that the projected Hessian matrix \(Z^{T}GZ\) has a negative eigenvalue; that is, assume there exists a vector \(u\) such that \(u^{T}Z^{T}GZu<0\). Show that, if there exists a pair \((x^{*},\lambda^{*})\) satisfying (16.4), then \(x^{*}\) is a stationary point of (16.3) but not a local minimizer. Hint: Consider \(q(x^{*}+\alpha Zu)\) for \(\alpha \ne 0\), and use an expansion analogous to that in the proof of Theorem 16.2."}
